section: Exploring Substances: Acidic, Basic and Neutral (Mixed Difficulty)

questions.question: Which specific acid is found in an ant's sting?
questions.explanation: When an ant bites, it injects formic acid into the skin, which causes the burning sensation.

questions.question: What is the chemical name of 'Milk of Magnesia' and what is it used for?
questions.correct_answer: Magnesium hydroxide; used as an antacid
questions.explanation: It is a base that neutralizes excess hydrochloric acid in the stomach to relieve indigestion.

questions.question: Describe the color changes of China Rose indicator in acidic, basic, and neutral solutions.
questions.correct_answer: Acidic: Magenta (Dark Pink); Basic: Green; Neutral: No change
questions.explanation: China rose is a natural indicator that shows distinct color transitions across different pH levels.

questions.question: Why is factory waste neutralized before being discharged into water bodies?
questions.correct_answer: To prevent the acids in the waste from killing fish and other aquatic organisms
questions.explanation: Most factory wastes are highly acidic; adding basic substances makes the water safe for aquatic life.

questions.question: What happens when a drop of dilute Hydrochloric acid is added to Blue Litmus paper?
questions.explanation: Acids have the property of turning blue litmus paper red.

questions.question: How should a farmer treat soil that has become too basic (alkaline)?
questions.explanation: Organic matter releases acids which neutralize the basic nature of the soil, unlike quicklime which is used for acidic soil.

questions.question: What are the products of a neutralization reaction?
questions.explanation: The reaction is exothermic (releases heat) and results in the formation of a salt and water.

questions.question: Name the acid found in Vinegar and the acid found in Tamarind.
questions.correct_answer: Vinegar: Acetic acid; Tamarind: Tartaric acid
questions.explanation: These are organic acids naturally found in different food items.

questions.question: Is a solution of common salt acidic, basic, or neutral?
questions.explanation: Common salt (Sodium Chloride) does not change the color of any indicator, meaning it is neutral.

questions.question: What is the effect of basic solutions on Turmeric indicator?
questions.correct_answer: It turns the yellow indicator reddish-brown
questions.explanation: Turmeric is a natural indicator that stays yellow in acids but changes color in bases (like soap).

notes.title: Litmus Source
notes.content: Litmus is a natural dye extracted from lichens and is the most commonly used indicator.

notes.title: Neutralization Equation
notes.content: Acid + Base → Salt + Water (Heat is evolved).

notes.title: Indigestion Remedy
notes.content: Indigestion is caused by too much HCl in the stomach; it is treated with bases like Magnesium Hydroxide or Baking Soda.

facts.fact: Acids are sour, while bases are bitter and soapy to touch.
Phenolphthalein is a synthetic indicator that is pink in base and colorless in acid.
Calmamine solution (Zinc Carbonate) is used to treat ant bites.
